entries.citation: Mercik, Zaremba, and Demir (2026), Crypto Factor Zoo; spread estimators from Corwin and Schultz (2012) and Abdi and Ranaldo (2017)
entries.market_in_source: broad spot cryptocurrency cross-section using exchange-aggregated market data
entries.source_sample: 565 active and inactive cryptocurrencies, January 2018 to July 2024; CryptoCompare OHLC and volume across more than 250 exchanges; CoinMarketCap market value
entries.signal: 30-day turnover volatility or the simple average of 30-day Corwin-Schultz and two-day-corrected Abdi-Ranaldo OHLC spread estimates
entries.portfolio_rule: quartile sort; long low characteristic and short high characteristic
entries.weighting: report equal-weighted and point-in-time market-cap value-weighted portfolios separately
entries.expected_direction: low turnover volatility minus high turnover volatility; low estimated spread minus high estimated spread
entries.primary_endpoint: net weekly low-minus-high return with dependence-aware inference and direct L2 proxy validation
entries.target_scope: current registered OKX perpetual pool under lagged large/liquid point-in-time eligibility
blockers: current-live OKX universe is survivor conditioned and much narrower than the source universe
OKX-only volume is not the source paper's exchange-aggregated volume
one L2 day cannot validate the OHLC proxy across regimes
point-in-time contract and market-cap alignment must remain lagged
entries.permitted_next_action: validate the frozen 30-day estimators on predeclared multi-regime L2 dates; only then freeze one canonical path and at most two mechanism-based adaptations

entries.citation: Liu, Tsyvinski, and Wu (2022), Common Risk Factors in Cryptocurrency
entries.source_sample: 2014-2018; coins with price, volume, and market capitalization; market cap at least USD 1 million; active and defunct coins
entries.portfolio_rule: bottom 30 percent, middle 40 percent, top 30 percent
entries.weighting: point-in-time market-cap value weighted within each portfolio
entries.expected_direction: long top momentum, short bottom momentum
entries.primary_endpoint: weekly value-weighted top-minus-bottom net return with dependence-aware mean inference
entries.target_scope: full panel and above-median point-in-time market-cap subpool
blockers: current survivor-conditioned perpetual universe cannot reproduce the original broad spot universe
entries.permitted_next_action: run one frozen method-faithful perpetual-universe adaptation before any AI extension; do not call it an exact universe replication

entries.citation: Zaremba et al. (2022), Up or down? Short-term reversal, momentum, and liquidity effects in cryptocurrency markets
entries.signal: last-day return conditioned on contemporaneously measurable size and liquidity
entries.portfolio_rule: predeclared liquidity/size segments; do not pool opposite regimes
entries.weighting: report equal- and tradeability-aware implementations separately
entries.expected_direction: reversal in illiquid assets; momentum in the largest and most tradeable assets
entries.primary_endpoint: conditional next-day rank IC within the preregistered liquidity segment
blockers: top-40 liquid perpetual universe cannot identify the source paper's illiquid majority
entries.permitted_next_action: test large/liquid daily momentum only; do not claim a full reversal replication without a broad point-in-time universe

entries.citation: Chi et al. (2023), An Empirical Investigation on Risk Factors in Cryptocurrency Futures
entries.source_sample: OKEx major cryptocurrencies, November 2017 to March 2021
entries.signal: futures basis using 1, 3, 5, and 7 period lookbacks; five-day lookback is the main displayed specification
entries.forward_horizon: one day primary, one week robustness
entries.portfolio_rule: high, middle, and low signal portfolios; high minus low
entries.weighting: source-specific portfolio construction; preserve separately from our generic rank weights
entries.expected_direction: higher basis predicts higher dated-futures return premium in the source design
entries.primary_endpoint: net high-minus-low return and alpha conditional on market and competing factor returns
blockers: our instrument is a perpetual swap with funding payments and no maturity
perp-minus-spot basis is not the paper's dated-futures basis
entries.permitted_next_action: use the paper to prioritize basis, but label a perp basis test as a mechanism adaptation rather than a replication

entries.citation: Gornall, Rinaldi, and Xiao (2025), Perpetual Futures and Basis Risk: Evidence from Cryptocurrency
entries.signal: basis dislocation under constrained arbitrage and speculative demand
entries.portfolio_rule: not specified as a canonical cross-sectional alpha replication
entries.expected_direction: positive basis and positive funding identify long-spot/short-perp convergence pressure and funding receipts; they do not identify a standalone future perp-return direction
entries.primary_endpoint: net two-leg basis convergence plus realized funding after spot and perpetual execution costs
entries.permitted_next_action: run one bounded long-spot/short-perp mechanism batch; do not infer a free-standing funding direction from this source

entries.citation: Ackerer, Hugonnier, and Jermann (2026), Perpetual Futures Pricing
entries.market_in_source: linear, inverse, and quanto perpetual futures
entries.source_sample: no-arbitrage theory with cryptocurrency contract examples
entries.signal: perpetual-spot deviation interpreted jointly with the funding specification
entries.portfolio_rule: replication and convergence arguments use both primitive spot assets and the perpetual contract
entries.expected_direction: a positive perpetual premium makes the short perpetual side attractive through the premium-linked funding transfer; this is a pair mechanism, not an outright return forecast
entries.primary_endpoint: two-leg replication or convergence cash flow including funding
blockers: borrowing rates and margin liquidation paths are unavailable for a full no-arbitrage replication
entries.permitted_next_action: use a conservative unlevered two-leg historical mechanism adaptation and retain a claim ceiling below deployable arbitrage

entries.source_sample: current product specification; historical realized-rate events retain the actual cash-flow record
entries.signal: realized funding is a sparse settlement event; positive rates transfer cash from long perpetual positions to short positions
entries.formation_frequency: 1, 2, 4, or 8 hour settlement interval depending on contract
entries.portfolio_rule: funding is earned or paid only while the perpetual position is open at settlement
entries.weighting: funding fee equals position value times realized funding rate
entries.expected_direction: positive funding is received by the short perpetual leg and negative funding is paid by it
entries.primary_endpoint: realized sparse funding cash flow with no forward fill in PnL
blockers: historical funding formula regimes differ, so a fixed formula must not be imposed retrospectively
entries.permitted_next_action: sum observed realized events over each held interval and infer neither missing settlements nor a fixed eight-hour schedule

entries.citation: Novy-Marx and Velikov (2016), A Taxonomy of Anomalies and Their Trading Costs
entries.signal: same frozen economic signal with a wider hold region than entry region
entries.portfolio_rule: 10/20 sS for mid-turnover and 10/50 sS for high-turnover strategies: enter in the most extreme 10 percent and retain while inside the most extreme 20 or 50 percent
entries.weighting: buy/hold spread applied to existing portfolio construction
entries.expected_direction: none; hysteresis changes execution and turnover, not the economic signal sign
entries.primary_endpoint: net return improvement attributable to lower turnover after preserving gross signal exposure
blockers: equity percentiles are an execution-method adaptation when applied to crypto perpetual pairs
entries.permitted_next_action: allow one frozen 10/20 and 10/50 execution-rescue comparison per already-frozen mechanism; do not optimize the bands from our outcomes

entries.citation: Garleanu and Pedersen (2013), Dynamic Trading with Predictable Returns and Transaction Costs
entries.market_in_source: general dynamic portfolio choice with predictable returns
entries.source_sample: closed-form theoretical model with signals of different mean-reversion speeds
entries.signal: trade partially toward a forward-looking aim portfolio
entries.portfolio_rule: combine the existing position with the current aim rather than jumping fully to the frictionless target
entries.expected_direction: none; the method constrains execution speed only
blockers: the current pilot does not estimate a structural signal-decay model
entries.permitted_next_action: use the paper as support for hysteresis and partial adjustment; do not estimate a bespoke optimal speed after viewing the frozen audit split

entries.citation: Pyo and Jang (2026), Revisiting the Low-Volatility Anomaly in Cryptocurrency Markets; adversarial prior from Burggraf and Rudolf (2021)
entries.source_sample: 432 spot cryptocurrencies, January 2018 to November 2025; earlier contrary evidence covers more than 1,000 cryptocurrencies from 2013 to 2019
entries.signal: standard deviation of daily log returns over the preceding two or three months
entries.portfolio_rule: equal-weighted long lowest-volatility quintile and short highest-volatility quintile
entries.weighting: equal weighted within each extreme quintile
entries.expected_direction: low volatility minus high volatility, while treating the earlier opposite result as an adversarial prior
entries.primary_endpoint: net monthly-rebalanced low-minus-high spot return and monthly rank IC on IS and Validation
entries.target_scope: current registered OKX pool under lagged point-in-time large/liquid eligibility
blockers: the registered pool is current-live and therefore survivor conditioned
the source universe is much broader than the approximately 40 eligible OKX assets
historical observations through November 2025 overlap the source paper's sample
a short leg implemented with perpetuals would require a separate funding and execution audit
entries.permitted_next_action: run exactly two frozen 60-day and 90-day month-end spot-return adaptations; seal the post-November-2025 audit unless selection evidence is positive; do not generate intramonth or extra-window variants

entries.citation: Harvey, Liu, and Saretto (2020), An Evaluation of Alternative Multiple Testing Methods for Finance Applications
entries.expected_direction: more outcome-seen hypotheses require stronger evidence
entries.primary_endpoint: predeclared family-level false discovery control for screening and stricter final promotion inference
entries.permitted_next_action: use FDR for a low-stakes discovery screen and retain strict multiplicity controls for final promotion

entries.expected_direction: separate hypothesis research from deterministic implementation and evaluation
entries.permitted_next_action: borrow modular agent roles; do not treat reported returns as evidence for our crypto factors

entries.citation: Huang et al. (2026), From Hypotheses to Factors: Constrained LLM Agents in Cryptocurrency Markets
entries.expected_direction: append-only experiments, point-in-time DSL, and deterministic evaluation reduce uncontrolled agent search
entries.primary_endpoint: auditability of both successful and failed hypotheses
blockers: author-reported performance is not independent evidence
entries.permitted_next_action: borrow constraints only; keep frozen audit-split feedback more restrictive than the paper
